title: RADAR: Robust Anomaly Detection and Recognition. A Unified and Reproducible Framework Across Tabular, Time-Series, and Federated Data
abstract: Anomaly detection is a critical task across multiple domains. This paper presents RADAR, a unified and reproducible framework for robust anomaly detection and recognition that handles tabular data, time-series, and federated learning scenarios. Our framework provides standardized evaluation protocols and benchmarking capabilities for comparing anomaly detection methods across diverse data types.

citations.apa: Bello-García, B., Aguilera-Martos, I., Rodríguez-Barroso, N., Herrera, F., & Luengo, J. (2026). RADAR: Robust Anomaly Detection and Recognition. A Unified and Reproducible Framework Across Tabular, Time-Series, and Federated Data. Neurocomputing.
citations.mla: Bello-García, B., I. Aguilera-Martos, N. Rodríguez-Barroso, F. Herrera, and J. Luengo. "RADAR: Robust Anomaly Detection and Recognition. A Unified and Reproducible Framework Across Tabular, Time-Series, and Federated Data." Neurocomputing, 2026.

contributions: Benchmarking capabilities across diverse data types
